_meta.source: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy — Steven Hayes
_meta.purpose: ACT hexaflex processes for clinical cartridge design
_meta.note: ACT focuses on psychological flexibility — the ability to be present, open up, and do what matters

core_goal: Psychological flexibility — contacting the present moment fully as a conscious human being, and based on what the situation affords, changing or persisting in behavior in the service of chosen values.

hexaflex.name: Acceptance
hexaflex.definition: Actively embracing private events (thoughts, feelings, sensations) without trying to change their form or frequency.
hexaflex.key_metaphor: Quicksand — struggling makes it worse; opening up and spreading out lets you float.

exercises.name: Physicalizing
exercises.instruction: Give the feeling a shape, color, size, texture. Observe it as an object rather than a threat.
exercises.game_mapping: Character encounters an emotion as a visible entity in the game world — they must observe it, not fight it.

exercises.name: Expansion
exercises.instruction: Breathe into the feeling. Make room for it. Don't try to push it away or shrink it.
exercises.game_mapping: A quest obstacle that grows larger when attacked but shrinks when accepted.

exercises.name: Willingness Scale
exercises.instruction: Rate willingness to have this feeling from 0-10. Notice what happens to the feeling when willingness increases.
exercises.game_mapping: NPC asks: 'How willing is your character to sit with this feeling right now?'

hexaflex.npc_design: An NPC who faces pain openly — doesn't avoid or suppress, but moves through it with dignity.

hexaflex.name: Cognitive Defusion
hexaflex.opposite: Cognitive fusion (believing thoughts are literal truth)
hexaflex.definition: Learning to step back from thoughts and see them as just thoughts — not commands, not truths, just mental events.
hexaflex.key_metaphor: Passengers on the Bus — you're the driver, thoughts are noisy passengers. They can yell, but you choose where to drive.

exercises.name: I'm having the thought that...
exercises.instruction: Prefix every difficult thought with 'I'm having the thought that...' Notice how it changes.
exercises.game_mapping: NPC narrates the character's thoughts in third person: 'Your character is having the thought that they'll fail.'

exercises.name: Silly Voice
exercises.instruction: Say the thought in a silly voice (cartoon character, song). Notice how it changes the grip.
exercises.game_mapping: A playful NPC who restates the character's fear in a lighthearted way — not dismissive, but defusing.

exercises.name: Leaves on a Stream
exercises.instruction: Visualize thoughts as leaves floating down a stream. Place each thought on a leaf and watch it drift by.
exercises.game_mapping: A meditation location in the game world — a river where thoughts appear as floating objects.

exercises.name: The Mind Radio
exercises.instruction: Imagine your mind is a radio that plays unhelpful programs. You can turn down the volume without turning it off.
exercises.game_mapping: An in-game 'noise' mechanic — intrusive thoughts as ambient sound the character can lower but not silence.

hexaflex.npc_design: A wise NPC who speaks about thoughts as weather — they come and go, they're not the sky.

hexaflex.name: Being Present
hexaflex.opposite: Dwelling on past or fearing future
hexaflex.definition: Contacting the present moment fully and without defense — experiencing what is here and now.
hexaflex.key_metaphor: Life is a stage performance — you can only act in this scene, not the ones before or after.

exercises.name: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding
exercises.instruction: Name 5 things you see, 4 you hear, 3 you can touch, 2 you smell, 1 you taste.
exercises.game_mapping: When character is overwhelmed, NPC guides a sensory inventory of the current game location.

exercises.name: Mindful Check-In
exercises.instruction: Pause and notice: What am I feeling right now? What am I thinking? What am I doing?
exercises.game_mapping: Regular check-in mechanic where the game pauses and asks the character to notice their state.

exercises.name: Dropping Anchor
exercises.instruction: ACE: Acknowledge what's showing up, Connect with your body, Engage in what you're doing.
exercises.game_mapping: A 'grounding' action available in every location — the character plants their feet and anchors.

hexaflex.npc_design: An NPC who always speaks in the present tense — always noticing what's happening now.

hexaflex.name: Self-as-Context
hexaflex.definition: The observing self — the part of you that is aware of all your experiences but is not defined by any of them.
hexaflex.key_metaphor: The Sky and the Weather — you are the sky, not the weather. Storms pass through you but you remain.

exercises.name: Observer Exercise
exercises.instruction: Notice that there is a part of you observing all your thoughts and feelings. That part has been there your whole life, unchanged.
exercises.game_mapping: A quest where the character gains the ability to 'see themselves from above' — an observer perspective moment.

exercises.name: Continuous You
exercises.instruction: Remember yourself at 5, 15, 25 years old. Notice that the 'you' who was aware then is the same 'you' now.
exercises.game_mapping: A flashback mechanic where the character sees their past selves — and recognizes continuity.

exercises.name: Chessboard Metaphor
exercises.instruction: Your thoughts and feelings are chess pieces fighting. But you are the board — you hold all the pieces without being any of them.
exercises.game_mapping: An NPC who presents conflict as a game board — the character realizes they're bigger than any single move.

hexaflex.npc_design: A narrator figure who can see the character's full story — past, present, potential futures.

hexaflex.name: Values
hexaflex.opposite: Dominance by avoidance, fusion, or social compliance
hexaflex.definition: Chosen life directions — not goals (which can be achieved) but qualities of ongoing action.
hexaflex.key_metaphor: Compass — values are the direction, not the destination. You never 'arrive' at a value.

exercises.name: Values Card Sort
exercises.instruction: Sort value cards into 'Very Important', 'Quite Important', 'Not Important'. Pick your top 5.
exercises.game_mapping: Character creation phase — player sorts value cards to shape the game world.

exercises.name: 80th Birthday Speech
exercises.instruction: Imagine your 80th birthday. What would you want people to say about you? What kind of life would you want to have lived?
exercises.game_mapping: An NPC 'oracle' who asks: 'What do you want your character's story to be about when it's done?'

exercises.name: The Tombstone Question
exercises.instruction: What would you want written on your tombstone? Not achievements — qualities.
exercises.game_mapping: A game ending that reflects the values the character lived by — different endings for different values.

hexaflex.npc_design: An NPC who asks about direction, not destination: 'Where is your character heading — not where are they going?'
hexaflex.game_integration: Values should shape the game world from character creation onward. Every major choice connects back to a value.

hexaflex.name: Committed Action
hexaflex.definition: Taking effective action guided by values — even when it's hard, even when uncomfortable thoughts and feelings show up.
hexaflex.key_metaphor: The journey — you walk in the direction of your values, step by step. You will stumble. You get up and keep walking.

exercises.name: SMART Values Goals
exercises.instruction: Set Specific, Meaningful, Adaptive, Realistic, Time-framed goals connected to values.
exercises.game_mapping: Quest objectives are always framed as value-connected actions, not arbitrary tasks.

exercises.name: Willingness + Action
exercises.instruction: Ask: Am I willing to have [uncomfortable feeling] in order to do [valued action]? Then do it.
exercises.game_mapping: Quest choices that require the character to face discomfort to achieve something meaningful.

exercises.name: Pattern Breaking
exercises.instruction: Identify a behavior pattern that's not working. Choose one small thing to do differently.
exercises.game_mapping: A quest arc where the character's old pattern fails, and they must try a new approach.

hexaflex.npc_design: An NPC mentor who says: 'You don't have to feel ready. You just have to take the next step.'

psychological_flexibility_matrix.description: The ACT Matrix — a simple visual tool that maps behavior along two dimensions

axes.vertical: Toward what matters (top) vs. Away from unwanted experience (bottom)

quadrants.top_left: What matters to me (values, people, qualities)
quadrants.bottom_right: Actions moving away from pain (avoidance behaviors)

psychological_flexibility_matrix.game_mapping: Every quest choice can be mapped to the matrix — is the character moving toward or away? This is therapeutic data for the dashboard.
